Star Symbols are for On the Cuss of things 'NM'=New Moon 'O'=Full Moon 'LQ'=Last Quarter 'FQ'=First Quarter A Few of Todays and Tommorows Famous Birthdays and Then The Rest
Start Day Day This Time Days To Go Start Date Years From Start Date Same Dayer Count Age For Next Same Dayer Born On A Leap Year Days After A New Moon Next/Last B-Day On A Full Moon Description

Tue Sat 0 01-08-1009 1000 142 1009 No NM 11 N/A -- Exact 1000 Years Ago --
Mon Sat 0 01-08-1498 511 74 514 No NM 13 N/A Christopher Columbus landed on "Isla Santa" (Venezuela).
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1509 500 71 502 No NM 14 N/A -- Exact 500 Years Ago --
Thu Sat 0 01-08-1619 390 56 395 No O 20 N/A The first black Americans (20) land at Jamestown, VA.
Thu Sat 0 01-08-1709 300 44 305 No LQ 24 N/A -- Exact 300 Years Ago --
Wed Sat 0 01-08-1770 239 35 243 No FQ 9 N/A B Day:-William Clark, explored Pacific Northwest with Lewis.
Mon Sat 0 01-08-1774 235 34 238 No LQ 23 N/A Oxygen was isolated from air successfully by chemist Carl Wilhelm and scientist Joseph Priestly.
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1779 230 32 232 No O 18 N/A B DAY:-Frances Scott Key - Attorney/Songwriter
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1779 230 32 232 No O 18 N/A B Day:-Francis Scott Key, wrote the 'Star-Spangled Banner'.
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1779 230 32 232 No O 18 N/A Francis Scott Key was born. He was an American composer, attorney, poet, and social worker. He was the composer of the poem "Defence of Fort McHenry" which later became known as the "Star-Spangled Banner."
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1790 219 30 221 No O 19 N/A The first U.S. census was completed. The population of the 17 states was 3,929,214.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1809 200 30 209 No O 19 N/A -- Exact 200 Years Ago --
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1815 194 29 203 No LQ 25 N/A B Day:-Richard Henry Dana, spent 2 years before the mast.
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1818 191 27* 198 No LQ 28 N/A Maria Mitchell was born. She was the first female professional astronomer and the first women to be elected into the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1819 190 27 192 No FQ 9 N/A B DAY:-Herman Melville - Writer
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1819 190 27 192 No FQ 9 N/A Herman Melville was born, the author of "Moby Dick."
Fri Sat 0 01-08-1834 175 25 181 No LQ 25 N/A Slavery was outlawed in the British empire with an emancipation bill.
Wed Sat 0 01-08-1866 143 21 147 No O 19 N/A B DAY:-Claude Bragden - Architect
Fri Sat 0 01-08-1873 136 20 142 No FQ 7 N/A Andrew S. Hallidie successfully tested a cable car. The design was done for San Francisco, CA.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1876 133 20 142 Yes FQ 10 N/A Colorado became the 38th state to join the United States.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1893 116 18 125 No O 18 N/A Shredded wheat was patented by Henry Perky and William Ford.
Wed Sat 0 01-08-1894 115 17 119 No LQ* 0 N/A The first Sino-Japanese War erupted. The dispute was over control of Korea.
Thu Sat 0 01-08-1907 102 15 107 No O * 21 1966 2023 Event:-Bank of Italy opens 1st branch at 3433 Mission Street.
Thu Sat 0 01-08-1907 102 15 107 No O * 21 1966 2023 The U.S. Army established an aeronautical division that later became the U.S. Air Force.
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1909 100 15 102 No O * 15 1966 2023 -- Exact 100 Years Ago --
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1914 95 13* 102 No FQ 8 1966 2023 Germany declared war on Russia at the beginning of World War I.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1933 76 12 85 No FQ 9 1966 2023 B DAY:-Dom Deluise - Actor
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1936 73 10* 80 Yes FQ 13 1966 2023 Adolf Hitler presided over the Olympic games as they opened in Berlin.
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1936 73 10* 80 Yes FQ 13 1966 2023 B DAY:-Yves St. Laurent - Designer
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1942 67 9* 74 No O 18 1966 2023 B DAY:-Jerry Garcia - Musician
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1943 66 10 68 No NM * 0 1966 2023 Several deaths occurred in a race-related riot in Harlem, New York City.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1944 65 10 74 Yes FQ 11 1966 2023 In Warsaw, Poland, an uprising against Nazi occupation began. The revolt only lasted two months.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1944 65 10 74 Yes FQ 11 1966 2023 World War II -- Uprising to free Warsaw begins
Thu Sat 0 01-08-1946 63 9 68 No NM 3 1966 2023 In the U.S., the Atomic Energy Commission was established.
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1953 56 8* 63 No O 20 1966 2023 The first aluminum-faced building was completed. It was the first of this type in America.
Wed Sat 0 01-08-1956 53 8 57 Yes LQ 23 1966 2023 The Social Security Act was amended to provide benefits to disabled workers aged 50-64 and disabled adult children.
Thu Sat 0 01-08-1957 52 8 57 No NM 4 1966 2023 The North American Air Defense Command (NORAD) was created by the United States and Canada.
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1959 50 7* 57 No LQ 25 1966 2023 -- Exact Fifty Years Ago --
Mon Sat 0 01-08-1966 43 6 46 No O * 14 1966 2023 Fifteen people were shot and killed and 31 others were injured by Charles Joseph Whitman from a tower at the University of Texas at Austin. Whitman was killed in the tower.
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1971 38 6 40 No FQ 9 1966 2023 Space -- Apollo 15 finds rock from birth of Moon
Wed Sat 0 01-08-1973 36 6 40 No NM 2 1966 2023 The movie "American Graffiti" opened.
Fri Sat 0 01-08-1975 34 6 40 No LQ 22 1966 2023 The Helsinki accords pledged the signatory nations to respect human rights.
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1976 33 5 35 Yes NM 4 1966 2023 The Seattle Seahawks played their first (preseason) game. The Seahawks lost 27-20 to San Francisco.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1978 31 5 40 No LQ 26 1966 2023 Pete Rose of the Cincinnati Reds ended his streak of hitting in 44 consecutive games.
Fri Sat 0 01-08-1986 23 4 29 No LQ 24 1966 2023 Bert Blyleven became only the 10th pitcher to strike out 3,000 batters in his career.
Fri Sat 0 01-08-1986 23 4 29 No LQ 24 1966 2023 John McEnroe and Tatum O'Neal were married.
Mon Sat 0 01-08-1988 21 3 24 Yes O 18 1966 2023 Martin Scorsese's "The Last Temptation of Christ" opened.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1989 20 4 29 No NM * 0 1966 2023 -- Exact Twenty Years Ago --
Thu Sat 0 01-08-1991 18 3 23 No O 20 1966 2023 Actress Hedy Lamar, 77, was arrested for shoplifting in Florida.
Sun Sat 0 01-08-1993 16 3 18 No FQ 12 1966 2023 Reggie Jackson was admitted into the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, NY.
Tue Sat 0 01-08-1995 14 3 23 No NM 4 1966 2023 Westinghouse Electric Corporation announced a deal to buy CBS for $5.4 billion.
Sat Sat 0 01-08-1998 11 1* 18 No FQ 8 1966 2023 The U.S. books and music chain Borders opens its first European outlet with a 40,000-square-foot store on London's Oxford Street.
